meta.name: Full i18n Wiring — Fix All Hardcoded English Strings
meta.description: All dictionary keys exist in 10 languages but components hardcode English. This prompt wires every component and page to read from the i18n dictionary. No new translation work needed — everything is already translated.

i18n_system.client_hook: useDictionary() from src/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider.tsx — returns Dictionary type
i18n_system.server_function: getCurrentDictionary() from src/lib/i18n/getDictionary.ts — returns { dict, locale }
i18n_system.provider: DictionaryProvider wraps the entire app in src/app/layout.tsx — all client components have access
i18n_system.dictionary_type: Inferred from src/lib/i18n/dictionaries/en.ts export type Dictionary = typeof en
i18n_system.translation_policy: Industry-standard fintech terms (Sharpe, Sortino, ATR, FVG, BOS, CHoCH, Stop Loss, Take Profit, Backtest, Drawdown, PnL, R-multiple) stay in English everywhere. Only natural-language strings get translated.

context.root_cause: Components don't import useDictionary() or receive dict props. The keys exist in all 10 language files but are unused. Nav/footer show correct language because layout.tsx wires them, but child components ignore the dictionary.

steps.title: Wire BacktestForm.tsx to i18n
instructions: Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
At top of component function, add: const dict = useDictionary(); const t = dict.backtest.form;
Replace ALL hardcoded form labels with dictionary references:
'Configuration' → t.configHeading
'Custom' → t.customBadge
'Quick pick' → t.quickPickLabel
'Symbol' → t.symbolLabel
'BTCUSDT' (placeholder) → t.symbolPlaceholder
'Any Binance spot pair...' → t.symbolHelp
'Timeframe' → t.timeframeLabel
'15 minutes'/'30 minutes'/'1 hour'/'4 hours'/'1 day' → t.timeframes['15m'] etc.
'Start Date' → t.startDateLabel
'End Date' → t.endDateLabel
'Initial Capital (USD)' → t.initialCapitalLabel
'Advanced parameters (V5 defaults)' → t.advancedToggle
Advanced field labels: 'Risk %' → t.advancedFields.riskPct, 'Max position %' → t.advancedFields.maxPositionPct, etc.
'Reset to V5 defaults' → t.resetDefaults
'Run Backtest' → t.runButton
'Running…' → t.runningButton
steps.verify: Switch language in browser, form labels should translate

steps.title: Wire TradeTable.tsx to i18n
instructions: Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
At top of component: const dict = useDictionary(); const t = dict.backtest.table;
Replace hardcoded strings:
'No trades generated.' → t.noTrades
'Trade Log · {count} trades' → `${t.heading} · ${trades.length} ${t.tradesSuffix}`

Column headers '#','Side','Entry Date','Entry $','Exit Date','Exit $','PnL $','PnL %','R','Bars','Exit' → t.headers.id, t.headers.side, t.headers.entryDate, t.headers.entryPrice, t.headers.exitDate, t.headers.exitPrice, t.headers.pnl, t.headers.pnlPct, t.headers.rMultiple, t.headers.bars, t.headers.exit
'Show all {count} trades' → `${t.showAll} ${sorted.length} ${t.tradesSuffix}`

steps.title: Wire MetricsPanel.tsx to i18n
instructions: Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
At top of component: const dict = useDictionary(); const t = dict.backtest.metricsPanel;
Replace 'Full metrics breakdown' heading → t.heading
Replace section titles: 'Returns' → t.sections.returns, 'Risk' → t.sections.risk, 'Trade summary' → t.sections.tradeSummary, 'Trade quality' → t.sections.tradeQuality, 'Costs' → t.sections.costs
Replace all metric row labels with t.labels.* — e.g. 'Total Return' → t.labels.totalReturn, 'Sharpe Ratio' → t.labels.sharpeRatio, etc.
The en.ts metricsPanel.labels object has all 30+ metric names. Match each hardcoded label to its dictionary key.

steps.title: Wire ExportToolbar.tsx to i18n
instructions: Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
At top of component: const dict = useDictionary(); const tExport = dict.backtest.results.exports; const tToast = dict.backtest.toast;
Replace button labels: 'Download CSV' → tExport.downloadCsv, 'Copy link' → tExport.copyLink, 'Chart PNG' → tExport.chartPng
Replace toast messages used with useToast():
'CSV downloaded' → tToast.csvDownloaded
'Link copied' → tToast.linkCopied
'Copy failed' → tToast.copyFailed
'Chart exported' → tToast.chartExported
'No chart to export' → tToast.noChart

steps.title: Wire CommandPalette.tsx to i18n
instructions: Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
Move the ITEMS array definition INSIDE the component function (after useDictionary call) so it can reference dict
At top of component: const dict = useDictionary(); const cp = dict.commandPalette;
Replace: 'Search or jump to…' placeholder → cp.placeholder
Replace navigation item labels: 'Home' → dict.nav.home, 'Backtest' → dict.nav.backtest, 'Forward Test' → dict.nav.live, 'About' → dict.nav.about
Replace group names: 'Navigate' → cp.groups.navigate, 'Quick run' → cp.groups.quickRun, 'External' → cp.groups.external (check if these keys exist in en.ts commandPalette section)
Replace: 'No results' → cp.empty
Replace footer hints: 'navigate' → cp.hints.navigate, 'select' → cp.hints.select, 'toggle' → cp.hints.toggle (if keys exist)

NOTE: Quick run labels like 'Run BTCUSDT 1H · 2023→today' contain dynamic data — keep the symbol/interval parts but translate 'Run' prefix if dict has a key for it, otherwise these can stay as-is since they are technical identifiers

steps.title: Wire StageProgress.tsx to i18n
instructions: This component receives a 'stages' prop with stage labels. Check how it's called from backtest/page.tsx.

Option A (preferred): If the parent (backtest/page.tsx) constructs the stages array, the parent should pass translated stage names from dict.backtest.progress.stages.fetch, dict.backtest.progress.stages.indicators, dict.backtest.progress.stages.backtest
Option B: Add useDictionary() to StageProgress itself and map stage keys to dictionary values
Choose whichever option requires fewer changes. The parent (backtest/page.tsx) is already being wired in step 9.

steps.title: Wire StickyKpiBar.tsx to i18n
instructions: Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
At top of component: const dict = useDictionary(); const t = dict.backtest.results.kpis;
Replace metric abbreviations in the KPI cells:
'Return' → t.totalReturn (or a shorter form if available)
'Sharpe' → t.sharpe
'Max DD' → t.maxDrawdown
'Win' → t.winRate
'PF' → t.profitFactor
'Trades' — check if dict has a key, otherwise keep as-is (it's a technical label)
'Net' — check if dict has a key
Note: These are abbreviated labels. If the dictionary only has full names, use the first word or abbreviate consistently.

steps.title: Wire LiveEquityChart.tsx to i18n
instructions: The component already receives a 'title' prop (good).
Replace the hardcoded 'Loading...' string (around line 147) — either:
Option A: Add a 'loadingText' prop and pass dict.live.loading from the parent
Option B: Import useDictionary() and use dict.live.loading directly
Option B is simpler. Add import, add const dict = useDictionary(); and replace 'Loading...' with dict.live.loading

steps.title: Wire backtest/page.tsx error messages to i18n
instructions: This is already 'use client'. Add useDictionary() if not present.
Find all error messages and replace:
'Invalid date range' → dict.backtest.errors.invalidDateRange
'Symbol is required (e.g., BTCUSDT)' → dict.backtest.errors.symbolRequired
'Not enough data: got' → dict.backtest.errors.notEnoughData
'candles, need at least' → dict.backtest.errors.notEnoughDataNeed
'Backtest failed' → dict.backtest.errors.generic
Also wire the progress/stage section: dict.backtest.progress.fetchingTitle, dict.backtest.progress.runningTitle
Also wire KPI labels: dict.backtest.results.kpis.*
Also wire empty state: dict.backtest.emptyState.*
Also wire the chart section titles: dict.backtest.charts.*

steps.title: Wire error.tsx to i18n
instructions: Error boundaries in Next.js are 'use client' components but they run outside the normal component tree when an error is thrown.

IMPORTANT: Check if useDictionary() works inside error.tsx. If DictionaryProvider is above the error boundary in layout.tsx (it is — layout wraps children in DictionaryProvider), then useDictionary() should work.
Add: import { useDictionary } from '@/lib/i18n/DictionaryProvider'
Replace: 'Something went wrong' → dict.errors.title (check en.ts for exact key path)
Replace: 'An unexpected error occurred...' → dict.errors.body
Replace: 'digest' → dict.errors.digest
Replace: 'Try again' → dict.errors.tryAgain

steps.title: Wire backtest/error.tsx to i18n
instructions: Same pattern as step 10.
Replace: 'Backtest page crashed' → dict.errors.backtestTitle
Replace: 'Something went wrong while rendering the backtest page.' → dict.errors.backtestBody
Replace: 'Reload backtest' → dict.errors.reloadBacktest

steps.title: Wire LiveExitReasons.tsx to i18n
instructions: Replace hardcoded 'No data' with a prop or useDictionary().
Add a 'noData' key to live.tradeAnalytics.exitReasons in ALL 10 dictionary files:
en: 'No data', de: 'Keine Daten', es: 'Sin datos', fr: 'Aucune donnée', ja: 'データなし', ko: '데이터 없음', pt: 'Sem dados', ru: 'Нет данных', tr: 'Veri yok', zh: '暂无数据'
This is the ONLY new dictionary key needed in this entire implementation.

steps.title: Wire about/page.tsx to i18n
instructions: This is a server component. Add: const { dict } = await getCurrentDictionary();
Replace all page content (headings, paragraphs, section titles) with dict.about.* references.
Check en.ts for the about section structure. All keys should already exist.
If the about page has large prose blocks, they should each have a dictionary key.

steps.title: Wire changelog/page.tsx to i18n
instructions: This is a server component. Add: const { dict } = await getCurrentDictionary();
Replace page title, release headings, descriptions, roadmap labels with dict.changelog.* references.
Status labels ('current', 'in-progress', 'planned', 'researching') should use dictionary keys.
Check en.ts for the changelog section structure.

manual_checks: Open localhost in browser, set language to Turkish (TR)
Navigate to /backtest — form labels, button text, error messages should be in Turkish
Run a backtest — KPI labels, trade table headers, metrics panel labels in Turkish
Navigate to /live — entire page in Turkish (was already partially done)
Navigate to /about — page content in Turkish
Navigate to /changelog — release notes in Turkish
Press Cmd+K — command palette placeholder and navigation items in Turkish
Switch to Japanese — repeat spot checks
Switch back to English — everything should be in English

constraints: Do NOT add new dictionary keys (except 'noData' in step 12). All keys already exist in all 10 languages.
Do NOT change the DictionaryProvider or getDictionary infrastructure.
Do NOT translate technical terms (PnL, R, ATR, Sharpe, etc.) — project policy keeps these in English.
Preserve all existing functionality — only change string sources from hardcoded to dictionary.
For error boundaries (error.tsx), verify useDictionary() works before refactoring. If it doesn't, use a try-catch with English fallback.
Components that are NOT 'use client' must use getCurrentDictionary() (async server function), not useDictionary() (client hook).
The ITEMS array in CommandPalette must move inside the component function body to access the dictionary (currently it's a module-level constant).
